response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong full-stack engineer with an impressive career trajectory through Meta and Google, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project — nowhere near the 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development required. His primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. He has no demonstrated experience in capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or adjacent financial domains, and no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency. None of the optional desirables (Summit, Python, Kafka/MQ) appear in his profile. The gap between his actual skillset and the mandatory requirements is too wide to bridge with a brief interview.
strengths: Broad software engineering background spanning over 15 years with demonstrated ability to learn and apply new technologies quickly
CI/CD and DevOps experience (GitLab, Docker, Artifactory, trunk-based development) aligns with optional pipeline knowledge
Strong communication skills evidenced by public talks, workshops, and open-source community contributions
concerns: C++ experience is superficial (~6 months at NI plus a thesis project) — far below the mandatory 5–10 year hands-on requirement on Windows
Zero capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial-domain exposure across the entire career history
No evidence of SQL Server or Oracle proficiency; SQL is listed as a skill but context is web/MongoDB-centric
